title: Sanction Russian Nuclear Safety Violators Act of 2025
short_title: Sanction Russian Nuclear Safety Violators Act of 2025
stage_label: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subs
plain_summary: This bill lets the President punish people from other countries who make the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant in Ukraine unsafe or try to take control of it away from Ukraine. The punishments include freezing their money and property in the United States and stopping them from coming into the country. The President does not have to punish people who are trying to help Ukraine get control of the power plant back or who are providing humanitarian aid. This law is meant to protect the nuclear plant from being damaged during Russia's war with Ukraine.
last_action_text: Referred to the Committee on Foreign Affairs, and in addition to the Committee on the Judiciary, for a period to be subsequently determined by the Speaker, in each case for consideration of such provi

sections.title: Section 1  Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Sanction Russian Nuclear Safety Violators Act of two thousand twenty five.

sections.title: Section 2  Findings
sections.text: Congress has found these facts to be true. On February twenty fourth, two thousand twenty two, the Russian government led by Vladimir Putin started an unprovoked full scale war against Ukraine. Russian forces illegally took over the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant, which is the biggest nuclear power plant in Europe. They put Russian military equipment inside the power plant. Officials from Rosatom, which is Russia's state nuclear energy company, have been at the plant since March two thousand twenty two. They have asked for secret information about how the plant works. The International Atomic Energy Agency (an organization that monitors nuclear safety around the world) has said that having Rosatom officials there is a major safety problem. They could interfere with how the plant operates. The agency has also found that the power plant has damage in some places from bombing caused by Russia's invasion of Ukraine. The Director General has said that failing to remove military forces from the Zaporizhzhia nuclear power plant is playing with fire. In May two thousand twenty three, Russia moved citizens away from the area around Zaporizhzhia, including workers who operate the plant. This made concerns about the plant's safety and stability even worse.

sections.title: Section 3  Sanctions
sections.text: The President must impose punishment on any foreign person who has put the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Station in danger or weakened Ukraine's control of it since Russia started its unprovoked full scale invasion of Ukraine. The power plant is located in southeastern Ukraine. The punishments are these. First, the President can block and ban all business deals involving any property or money that belongs to the foreign person if that property or money is in the United States or comes under the control of a United States person. Second, any person who the Secretary of State or Secretary of Homeland Security knows or has reason to believe has endangered the power plant cannot enter the United States. They cannot get a visa or other papers to enter the United States. They cannot be admitted or allowed into the United States or get any other benefits under immigration law. Any visa or entry papers already given to such a person must be cancelled immediately. This cancellation happens right away and automatically cancels any other valid visa or entry papers the person has. There are some exceptions to these punishments. The travel ban does not apply if letting the person into the United States is needed to follow our agreement with the United Nations or other international obligations. Sanctions cannot be imposed on business deals for selling food, medicine, or medical equipment, providing humanitarian help, financial transactions for humanitarian help, or transporting goods and services needed for humanitarian operations. Sanctions do not apply to any foreign person trying to help Ukraine regain control of the Zaporizhzhia Nuclear Power Station or the surrounding area. The President can use all the powers given under the International Emergency Economic Powers Act to carry out these sanctions. Anyone who violates these rules faces the same penalties as someone who breaks that Act. The President can waive these sanctions for a foreign person if the President tells Congress at least fifteen days before the waiver takes effect that the waiver is vital to United States national security interests. In this section, appropriate congressional committees means the House Committee on Foreign Affairs and the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations. A foreign person means any individual or organization that is not a United States person. A United States person means a United States citizen, a permanent resident of the United States, a company organized under United States law including foreign branches of such companies, or a person in the United States.
